hinge: Gross isthmus fracture of amalgam is primarily caused by inadequate cavity preparation: insufficient depth/width, sharp internal line angles, or thin weak isthmus concentrating stress. High occlusal force may precipitate failure but usually acts on an already underprepared restoration.
From birth to 6 months, fluoride supplementation is 0 mg/day even in non-fluoridated communities. Fluoride supplementation tables only begin after 6 months of age based on water fluoride level.

MI one year ago (stable) and resolved hepatitis B do not mandate antibiotics for routine RCT. Short-course NSAID (naproxen ~5 days) is appropriate for post-endodontic pain control without unnecessary antibiotics.
Reverse smoking produces thermal and chemical irritation of the palate yielding a reactive keratotic/stomatitis-type white change, not a primarily malignant lesion on first presentation.
Fibromyalgia has no single pathognomonic laboratory test or biopsy finding; diagnosis is clinical and made by exclusion of other rheumatologic, neurologic, and endocrine causes of widespread pain.

New severe unilateral facial/jaw pain with systemic features (anorexia) in a patient with polymyalgia rheumatica spectrum raises giant cell arteritis until proven otherwise. This is a medical emergency requiring urgent steroids and referral — not simple myalgia.

Chlorhexidine's clinical effectiveness rests on substantivity — binding to oral surfaces and releasing slowly over hours for prolonged antimicrobial action. It does not work primarily through tissue dissolution or nonspecific cytotoxicity.

Surface (repair) resorption is the shallow, self-limiting cemental loss that appears as minimal external root contour change radiographically. Inflammatory and replacement resorption show more aggressive patterns. Image-dependent — confirm with provided radiograph.
A child who cannot sustain attention, constantly handles instruments, and loses focus fits the behavioral profile of attention-deficit/hyperactivity disorder among the coherent options listed.

Immediate palpitations after lidocaine with epinephrine most commonly indicate inadvertent intravascular injection, delivering a catecholamine bolus that causes tachycardia and palpitations. True systemic toxicity develops more gradually.

Combination syndrome pattern (maxillary complete denture opposing mandibular anterior teeth + RPD) produces a flabby anterior maxillary ridge, epulis fissuratum, and mandibular anterior attrition when the upper prosthesis is removed.

A painful submandibular swelling with a firm palpable mass and a radiopaque density on imaging is classic sialolithiasis. Sialadenosis is a non-inflammatory, non-stone enlargement; a duct cyst is not radiopaque.

For a single anterior implant (#11), prosthetically driven planning is essential: diagnostic casts with wax-up and a surgical template ensure proper 3D implant position matching the final crown restoration.

Pain on release of biting force (rebound pain) is the classic clinical finding for cracked tooth syndrome. Dentinal flexure closes on loading and snaps open on release, stimulating the pulp. VRF more often shows deep isolated probing and J-shaped bone defects.
Buccal gingival swelling over a primary molar indicates acute periapical abscess/infection, which is a definitive indication for extraction. Isolated percussion pain alone is less definitive.

Substandard RCT under a well-fitting cast post-core and crown makes orthograde retreatment highly destructive (post removal risks root fracture). Apical surgery with root-end resection and retrofill is the standard definitive path to seal the apex without disturbing the existing restoration.

Among psychiatric conditions, eating disorders (bulimia/purging) are classically linked to bilateral salivary gland enlargement and swelling due to recurrent vomiting and autonomic stimulation. Schizophrenia and bipolar disorder are not primary causes of submandibular swelling.

Uncontrolled diabetes with acute facial swelling and severe trismus, even with normal breathing, risks deep space infection and potential airway compromise. Immediate hospital admission with CT imaging and combined surgical/medical management is required.
Systemic corticosteroids raise blood glucose levels and can worsen glycemic control. Continued steroid use in a diabetic patient predictably increases HbA1c despite prior good control on metformin.
With expired insurance coverage, the ethical approach is to provide treatment only if the patient agrees to pay out of pocket. Billing as if insurance will retroactively cover care is improper.

Multiple punched-out radiolucencies of varying sizes in an adult skull/jaws are classic for multiple myeloma. Gorlin syndrome features multiple odontogenic keratocysts in younger patients, not innumerable punched-out skull lesions. These findings are not normal aging.

For deep caries possibly needing crown lengthening: take bitewing radiograph first to assess lesion depth and remaining tooth structure, then excavate caries to determine restorability and pulp status, then complete RCT if indicated, and finally perform surgical crown lengthening before the definitive buildup/crown. Radiographic assessment before excavation is essential to avoid rendering a tooth non-restorable.
Sodium hypochlorite (NaOCl) irrigant is strongly alkaline with pH ~11-12, higher than chlorhexidine (~5.5-7), normal saline (~7), or phosphoric acid (very low pH).
The predominant inorganic component of dentin is hydroxyapatite (calcium phosphate mineral). Collagen types I and V are the organic matrix components, not inorganic.

Diffuse erythematous gingivitis with oral mucosal ulcers in a primary herpetic pattern is managed with antivirals plus supportive care. Antibiotics alone do not treat HSV and corticosteroids can worsen viral disease.
Flumazenil is the competitive benzodiazepine receptor antagonist used to reverse midazolam sedation or overdose.

In preventive resin restorations, flowable composite penetrates narrow pits and fissures better due to lower viscosity and lower filler content than packable composites, ensuring complete seal of the prepared area.

For a 5-year-old with negative behavior and multiple occlusal lesions, fluoride varnish is applied quickly by painting/brushing with short chair time, no trays, low gag risk, and lower systemic ingestion risk compared to gel. Gel requires longer retention and patient cooperation.

Organic occlusion is synonymous with mutually protected occlusion: posteriors take occlusal load in MIP and protect anteriors, while anterior guidance discludes posteriors in excursive movements. Centric and eccentric describe jaw positions, not this protective scheme.
A positive Paul-Bunnell (heterophile antibody) test supports infectious mononucleosis caused by Epstein-Barr virus (EBV). It is not used to diagnose HSV, HPV, or SLE.
